question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single HTTP request with a body sized exactly at maxPayloadLogSize*10 (3000 bytes by default) to cause ContentCachingRequestWrapper to buffer the full payload in heap memory per request, and by sending a moderate number of concurrent such requests exhaust the JVM heap across multiple web3 nodes causing OOM crashes affecting ≥30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContentCachingRequestWrapper is allocated with buffer size maxPayloadLogSize*10 per request, and by sending a sustained stream of concurrent POST /api/v1/contracts/call requests each with a 3000-byte body, cause cumulative heap exhaustion on multiple mirror nodes simultaneously, shutting down ≥30% of them without brute-force volume?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the GZIPOutputStream allocation path in LoggingFilter.getContent() by crafting a request body slightly larger than maxPayloadLogSize (300 bytes), causing a ByteArrayOutputStream of size content.length()/4 plus GZIPOutputStream overhead to be allocated per request, and by sending a moderate number of concurrent requests exhaust CPU and heap memory across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request payload that is incompressible (e.g., random bytes or already-compressed data) of size just above maxPayloadLogSize, causing LoggingFilter to attempt GZIPOutputStream compression that produces output larger than the input, wasting CPU cycles per request, and by sending a moderate number of such requests cause CPU saturation across multiple web3 nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call request that triggers the maximum number of Hibernate SQL statements within the requestTimeout window (10 seconds), holding a database connection for the full duration, and by sending a moderate number of such concurrent requests exhaust the database connection pool across multiple web3 nodes, causing them to become unresponsive and effectively shutting down ≥30% of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 10-second requestTimeout default to hold a JVM thread and database connection per request for up to 10 seconds, and by sending a sustained moderate rate of concurrent requests exhaust the web server thread pool across multiple nodes, causing ≥30% of nodes to stop processing new requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user send contract call requests that each trigger a long-running EVM execution path, holding a thread for up to requestTimeout (10 seconds) before the statementInspector throws QueryTimeoutException, and by sending enough such requests in parallel exhaust the thread pool on multiple nodes, causing ≥30% of nodes to queue and eventually drop incoming requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the statementInspector only checks elapsed time at SQL statement boundaries, meaning a single long-running SQL query that does not yield control will never be interrupted by the timeout, allowing a crafted request to hold a database connection indefinitely and starve other requests across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer multiplication maxPayloadLogSize*10 in LoggingFilter where if maxPayloadLogSize is set near Integer.MAX_VALUE/10 via environment variable injection or misconfiguration, the multiplication overflows to a negative value causing ContentCachingRequestWrapper to throw an exception or allocate an unexpectedly large buffer, crashing the filter chain on all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DATA_PATTERN regex with a crafted payload containing a 'data' key followed by many alternating characters that force the .+ group to backtrack repeatedly, causing ReDoS (Regular Expression Denial of Service) that pins a CPU core per request and by sending a small number of such requests saturates all CPU cores across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request body that is exactly at the maxPayloadLogSize boundary (300 bytes) but contains content that after GZIP compression is still larger than maxPayloadLogSize, causing LoggingFilter to allocate both the GZIPOutputStream buffer and the Base64-encoded result in memory simultaneously, doubling memory pressure per request across all nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the 5xx error path in LoggingFilter.getContent() (where content is NOT truncated when status >= 500) by crafting a request that causes an internal server error, causing the full untruncated payload to be written to logs, and by sending many such requests with large payloads exhaust disk space on multiple nodes causing them to crash?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that deliberately triggers a 500 Internal Server Error response, bypassing the content truncation logic in LoggingFilter (which only truncates for non-5xx responses), and by sending a stream of such error-triggering requests with large payloads cause unbounded log growth that fills disk partitions on multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the QueryTimeoutException thrown by statementInspector() to cause database connection leaks if the exception is not properly handled by the connection pool, and by sending a sustained stream of requests that each time out, gradually exhaust the database connection pool across multiple nodes until they can no longer serve any requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user send contract call requests timed to complete just before the requestTimeout threshold, avoiding the QueryTimeoutException while still holding database connections for nearly the full 10 seconds, and by sending a moderate number of such requests in a sustained pattern exhaust the connection pool across multiple nodes without triggering rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user inject log forging characters (newlines, ANSI escape codes) into the request URI or body that get logged via LOG_FORMAT in LoggingFilter, causing log parsers or SIEM systems to misinterpret log entries, potentially triggering automated responses that shut down nodes flagged as compromised?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with payloads containing null bytes or malformed UTF-8 sequences that cause content.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8) in LoggingFilter.getContent() to throw an exception or produce unexpected output, causing the GZIPOutputStream to fail and potentially leaving resources unclosed, leading to resource leaks across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ByteArrayOutputStream is initialized with content.length()/4 in LoggingFilter.getContent(), and by sending a payload of exactly maxPayloadLogSize+1 bytes that is incompressible, cause the ByteArrayOutputStream to repeatedly resize its internal buffer during GZIP compression, causing excessive memory allocation and GC pressure across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that triggers a deeply recursive EVM execution path, causing the statementInspector to be invoked many times per request (once per SQL statement), and by sending a moderate number of such requests cause excessive overhead from repeated System.currentTimeMillis() calls and context lookups that degrade performance across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ContractCallContext.isInitialized() check in statementInspector() returns false for certain code paths, allowing SQL queries to run without any timeout enforcement, and by crafting requests that bypass context initialization cause unbounded database query execution that starves the connection pool across multiple nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a large number of requests with payloads that are exactly at the maxPayloadLogSize boundary, causing LoggingFilter to perform GZIP compression followed by Base64 encoding on every single request, creating sustained CPU load from compression operations that degrades throughput across multiple nodes to the point where ≥30% become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getMaxPayloadLogSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that StringUtils.deleteWhitespace() is called on the full cached request content before size comparison in LoggingFilter.getContent(), and by sending a payload consisting almost entirely of whitespace characters (e.g., spaces, tabs, newlines) cause the whitespace removal to produce a near-empty string while the original buffer still occupies maxPayloadLogSize*10 bytes of heap memory per request?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Properties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getRequestTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user send contract call requests that each trigger exactly one slow SQL query that completes just before the requestTimeout, causing the statementInspector to not throw but the request to still consume the full 10-second window, and by sending a sustained stream of such requests at a rate that fills the thread pool cause ≥30% of nodes to stop accepting new connections?
